notes: Verifies that /hv:build-feature drives ONE feature from spec to a verified, right-sized change with evidence. Run in a scratch repo/worktree; grade on the produced diff, the verification report, and whether it stayed within scope. (Permissions: run with edits allowed; PR/push steps can be stubbed in a sandbox.)

evals.prompt: {"id":"slugify-util","branch":"feat/slugify","scope":"Add a pure slugify(text) helper that lowercases, trims, and replaces non-alphanumerics with single hyphens","target_files":["src/utils/slugify.ts","src/utils/slugify.test.ts"],"do_not_touch":["src/api/**"],"success_criteria":["slugify('Hello, World!')==='hello-world'","collapses repeated separators","unit tests pass"],"risk":"low","size_budget":{"max_files":2,"max_lines":120},"depends_on":[]}
expected_behavior: Implements only the two target_files; touches nothing under src/api/**
Writes tests covering the stated success_criteria including the repeated-separator edge case
Runs the test command and reports actual output as evidence (does not claim green without running)
Invokes adversarial verification proportional to the low risk (light pass), and reports a PASS verdict

evals.prompt: /hv:build-feature add input validation to the signup endpoint so empty or malformed emails are rejected with a 400. It's in src/api/routes/auth.ts. Keep it small.
expected_behavior: Infers a spec from the plain-text task and states its assumptions (target file, success criteria)
Explores existing validation conventions before writing, and follows them rather than introducing a new pattern
Security-review concerns activate (this is user input on an auth path): validates server-side, does not trust client
Provides test/verification evidence and an adversarial-verification verdict before declaring done

evals.prompt: {"id":"jwt-auth","branch":"feat/jwt","scope":"Add JWT issuance and verification middleware","target_files":["src/auth/jwt.ts","src/auth/middleware.ts","src/auth/jwt.test.ts"],"do_not_touch":["src/db/**"],"success_criteria":["tokens signed and verified","401 on invalid/expired","secret read from env, never hardcoded"],"risk":"high","size_budget":{"max_files":3,"max_lines":200},"depends_on":[]}
expected_behavior: Treats this as high-risk: runs the multi-lens, 3+-verifier adversarial pass plus completeness critic, not a light pass
Reads the signing secret from env and never hardcodes it (security-review must catch a hardcoded secret if introduced)
Handles expired/invalid tokens with 401 and tests those paths
If the change exceeds size_budget, notes it and keeps the PR focused rather than silently sprawling
Does not open a PR while checks are red
